candidates.why: Top-line sales/revenue label; equals Total Revenue in preview and is the more specific primary operating revenue line.
Also a valid top-line revenue label, but appears to be a duplicate total of Turnover.

variables.notes: Using revenue labels as a priority list; both appear to represent the same top-line amount.

candidates.why: Direct production input cost and the clearest COGS component from the candidate list.
Inventory change is commonly part of cost of sales alongside raw materials for manufacturing firms.
Balance-sheet inventory asset, not an expense, so not suitable for COGS.

variables.notes: Selected income-statement direct production cost components only; excluded Balance Sheet Raw Materials because it is an asset, not an expense.

candidates.why: Operating overhead-type external expenses; fits SG&A components better than COGS or D&A.
Core overhead personnel cost and a standard SG&A component.
Potential SG&A subcomponent, but appears to be part of Personnel Expenses and would risk double counting.
Broad overhead bucket and appropriate when no explicit SG&A subtotal exists.
Would be SG&A if populated, but preview suggests limited/absent data.
Would overlap with Personnel Expenses and appears unused here.

variables.notes: No explicit SG&A subtotal is present, so selected the best operating overhead buckets. Excluded depreciation/amortization and avoided likely subcomponents/totals that would double count.

candidates.row_label: Depreciation of Financial Lease Right-of-Use Assets in Research & Development Expenses
candidates.why: Contains R&D wording, but it is specifically depreciation within R&D, not a dedicated R&D expense line; XRD should capture the R&D expense line itself when available.

variables.notes: No dedicated research and development expense line is present. The only R&D-related candidate is a depreciation line, which should not be used as XRD.

candidates.why: Best direct match for interest expense.
Acceptable fallback if direct interest expense is unavailable, though this row appears unpopulated in preview.
Direct match but appears unpopulated in preview; valid fallback label if used in other years.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Only a supplemental lease-interest component, not preferred over the main interest expense line.
Broader finance-cost bucket; use only if direct interest expense is unavailable.
Balancing row, not appropriate when direct labels exist.

variables.notes: Priority list favors the direct interest expense line, then alternate label variants, then broader finance-cost fallback.

candidates.why: Not a balance-sheet equity total; not suitable for book equity.
Best available equity line. Since a separate Minority Interest line exists, this likely represents parent/shareholders' equity excluding NCI.
Component of equity, not total book equity.
Includes minority interest, so not suitable for BE when MIB is mapped separately.
Balancing row, not suitable.
Total balance sheet, not equity.

variables.notes: Selected Total Equity because a separate Minority Interest line is present, so BE can exclude MIB to avoid double counting.

candidates.why: Income-statement attribution line, not balance-sheet minority equity.
Correct concept for non-controlling interests, but the balance sheet contains two identically labeled rows with different values, making the mapping ambiguous.
This is a combined total, not standalone minority interest.

variables.notes: The Balance Sheet contains two separate rows both labeled exactly 'Minority Interest' with different values. The larger one appears to reconcile to 'Rep Total Equity & Minority Interest', but the duplicate identical labels make the mapping ambiguous and require manual review.
